title: HCI 271 — Week 6 Weekly Reflection (Mapping from Data to Design)
intro: I'll walk you through 6 reflection areas from this week's template (Parts 1, 2, and 3). At each step you can ask to revise an earlier answer. Type STOP at any point to end early. When we finish, you'll get a PDF to upload to Canvas.
transition_template: Thanks — anything else on {{section_topic}} before we move on?
advance_template: Got it. Now let's switch to {{next_section_title}} — {{next_section_one_line}}.

sections.title: Key Concepts & Takeaways
sections.topic: the concept that stuck with you this week
sections.one_line: the single concept or skill from Week 6 that stood out
sections.opening_prompt: What's one idea from this week's data analysis session that actually stuck with you, and why?
sections.depth_probe: Can you anchor that in something concrete — a moment in lecture, a slide, a sticky note, or a piece of your team's data — that made it click?

sections.title: Methods in Practice
sections.topic: the synthesis method you actually applied this week
sections.one_line: a specific synthesis method you used (affinity diagramming, thematic coding, journey mapping, etc.)
sections.opening_prompt: Which synthesis method did you actually try with your team this week, and how did it feel in practice versus how it was described in lecture?
sections.depth_probe: Did you hit any of the NN/g pitfalls — clustering by topic instead of behavior, premature labelling, one person driving the wall, going abstract too fast? Pick one and tell me what happened.

sections.title: Knowledge Shift: Before vs. After
sections.topic: how your understanding changed this week
sections.one_line: what you thought before, what surprised you, what's still uncertain
sections.opening_prompt: Before this week, what did you assume about turning raw data into design direction?
sections.depth_probe: What specifically challenged that assumption — a piece of evidence, a teammate's point, a moment in the affinity wall?

fields.label: What I am still uncertain about

sections.title: Connection to the Capstone Project
sections.topic: how this week's learning lands in your team's project
sections.one_line: one specific way this week's learning will change your project going forward
sections.opening_prompt: How will what you learned this week change your team's research approach, your interview questions, or an emerging design decision?
sections.depth_probe: What's the smallest concrete change you'd make next week as a result?

sections.title: Planning & Execution
sections.topic: how your team planned and executed this week
sections.one_line: your team's plan, division of work, and adaptation
sections.opening_prompt: Did your team have a shared understanding of the week's goals before you started, or did that emerge as you went?
sections.depth_probe: If you adapted the plan mid-week, what triggered the change and was it the right call in hindsight?

sections.title: Roles & Contributions
sections.topic: who did what on your team this week
sections.one_line: list each teammate and their primary contribution this week
sections.opening_prompt: Let's capture what each teammate worked on this week. Who's on your team — list everyone, including yourself?
sections.depth_probe: Was the distribution of work equitable given each person's strengths? If not, what would you change?

fields.label: Was distribution equitable? If not, what would you change?

sections.collection_strategy: ask for the full roster up front, then walk member-by-member for contributions, then ask the equity question.

sections.title: Collaboration & Communication
sections.topic: how your team communicated and made decisions
sections.one_line: what worked, what was challenging, one actionable improvement
sections.opening_prompt: Tell me about one specific communication moment from this week that worked well — what exactly happened?
sections.depth_probe: Now flip it — what specifically broke down? Avoid 'communication was hard' — give me the concrete moment and its impact.

fields.label: One actionable improvement for next week (measurable)

sections.title: Team Health Check
sections.opening_prompt: Now five quick team-health ratings on a 1–5 scale (1 = strongly disagree, 5 = strongly agree), each with a one-sentence why. Ready?
sections.collection_strategy: ask justification first, then rating, for each dimension — to prevent default-fives.

fields.dimension: We had a clear, shared goal for the week.
Everyone's contributions were valued and heard.
We met our commitments and deadlines to each other.
I feel confident about our direction going into next week.

sections.title: Our Biggest Open Question
sections.topic: the one question you most need to answer next
sections.one_line: the open question (about users, problem space, methods, or team)
sections.opening_prompt: What is the single question — about your users, your problem, your method, or your team — that you most need to answer before you can move forward with confidence?
sections.depth_probe: Why that one specifically? What makes it the bottleneck right now?

sections.title: Our Commitment for Next Week
sections.topic: your concrete commitment for next week
sections.one_line: one specific commitment your team is making
sections.opening_prompt: Based on everything you just reflected on, what's one concrete commitment your team is making for next week — process change, research action, or design decision?
sections.depth_probe: How will you know you actually did it? What's the observable signal?

closing.behavior: After all sections have at least one student response and the student signals done, ask one feedback question about the bot itself, then emit [END] on its own line.
closing.feedback_prompt: Last thing — did this conversation surface more honest reflection than filling out the PDF would have, and what would make it work better next week?
